LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION AND FOR SANCTIONS - COURT DISCLOSURE: I AM A MEMBER OF THE MCFETRIDGE INN OF COURT. DEFENDANT ATTORNEY DONNA RUTTER IS ALSO A MEMBER AND I SEE HER OCCASIONALLY AT INN OF COURT MEETINGS. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT MY ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. I DO NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THIS FACT WOULD REASONABLY ENTERTAIN A DOUBT THAT I WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. RULING: DEFENDANT THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER CSR#6576, 415.597.5600. (302)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CINDY XU'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT THE RITZ CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 1: DEFENDANT SHALL ANSWER THIS INTERROGATORY FULLY. AS TO FORMER EMPLOYEES (NO LONGER EMPLOYED BY DEFENDANT), DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE LAST KNOWN CONTACT INFORMATION. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 2: DEFENDANT SHALL ANSWER THIS INTERROGATORY AS TO EMPLOYEES WORKING AT THE HOTEL FROM JANUARY 1, 2012 ONWARD. AS TO FORMER EMPLOYEES (NO LONGER EMPLOYED BY DEFENDANT), DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE LAST KNOWN CONTACT INFORMATION. SANCTIONS DENIED AS TO BOTH SIDES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES O. GEERHART; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RAUL GUILLERMO'S MOTION FOR ORDER QUASHING DEPOSITION SUBPOENA IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A COMPELLING INTEREST TO OVERRIDE MR. GUILLERMO'S RIGHT OF PRIVACY TO HIS EMPLOYMENT RECORDS AT A FORMER EMPLOYER FOR WHICH HE WORKED FIVE YEARS BEFORE THE INCIDENT IN QUESTION. SPECULATION THAT THE DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN IMPROPER CONDUCT AT THE PRIOR EMPLOYER DOES NOT ESTABLISH A COMPELLING NEED OR RELEVANCE. HARDING LAWSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 10 CAL. APP. 4TH 7. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Continued To Mar-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant The Ritz-Carlton Hotel Company, Llc'S Motion To Compel An Independent Medical Examination And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Kim, Esq., 650.697.8899, for plaintiff Cindy Qiwang Xu.
Jennifer Cotner, Esq., 415.464.4300, for defendant The Ritz-Carlton Hotel Company, LLC.

COURT DISCLOSURE: I am a member of the McFetridge Inn of Court. Defendant attorney Donna Rutter is also a member and I see her occasionally at Inn of Court meetings. This will not affect my ability to be fair and impartial in this case. I do not believe that a person aware of this fact would reasonably entertain a doubt that I would be able to be impartial.

RULING: At the beginning of the hearing, the Court issued its tentative ruling: Defendant the Ritz-Carlton Hotel Company, LLC's motion to compel an independent medical examination is GRANTED; request for sanctions is denied. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter CSR#6576, 415.597.5600.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena;
Off Calendar Mar-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEAN-PAUL LECLERCQ OF THE MAKAREM & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (310) 312-0299 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. GAIL F. FLATT OF PROVENCHER & FLATT LLP, TEL. NO. (707) 284-2380 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT RAUL GUILLERMO'S Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena is granted. The Court finds that Plaintiff has not shown a compelling interest to override Mr. Guillermo's right of privacy to his employment records at a former employer for which he worked five years before the incident in question. Speculation that the Defendant engaged in improper conduct at the prior employer does not establish a compelling need or relevance. Harding Lawson v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal. App. 4th 7. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/JPT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft The Ritz Carlton Hotel Company, Llc To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on Friday, January 10, 2014:
Michael H. Kim, Esq. [650.697.8899] appearing for Plaintiff;
Jennifer Cotner, Esq. of Miller Law Group [ 415.464.4300] appearing for Defendant

ARGUED - R U L I N G Re: PLAINTIFF CINDY XU'S Motion To Compel Defendant The Ritz Carlton Hotel Company, LLC To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One) And For Sanctions. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: As to Special Interrogatory No. 1: Defendant shall answer this interrogatory fully. As to former employees (no longer employed by defendant), defendant shall provide last known contact information. As to Special Interrogatory No. 2: Defendant shall answer this interrogatory as to employees working at the hotel from January 1, 2012 onward. As to former employees (no longer employed by defendant), defendant shall provide last known contact information. Sanctions denied as to both sides. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Charles O. Geerhart; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motiohn And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedigns
Off Calendar Apr-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL KIM, ESQ., 310.733.6303, FOR PLAINTIFF CINDY QIWANG XU.
JENNIFER COTNER, ESQ., 415.464.4300, FOR DEFENDANT THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT THE RITZ-CARLTON HOTEL COMPANY, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is denied. Plaintiff's harassment action is not within the scope of the parties' agreement to arbitrate. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
